findings.evidence: `rg -n "TauCeti\.Analysis\.InnerProductSpace\.LaxMilgram|solutionOfInner|solutionOfFunctional|existsUnique_forall_eq|continuousLinearEquivOfBilin" code/TauCeti/Analysis/PDE/CoercivePerturbation.lean ...` reports only `CoercivePerturbation.lean:9:public import TauCeti.Analysis.InnerProductSpace.LaxMilgram` and no use of its public declarations.
findings.fix: Drop this import, or make the dependency private only if some proof actually needs it; `IsCoercive` is already available through the existing PDE coercive-energy import chain.
findings.issue: `public import TauCeti.Analysis.InnerProductSpace.LaxMilgram` exposes the general Lax--Milgram solution layer to every importer of the PDE perturbation module, even though this file only states pointwise coercivity/estimate lemmas.

summary: The perturbation lemmas are scoped to the PDE target, but the new module publicly re-exports an unrelated Lax--Milgram solution API that it does not use.
